title: gymnadenia conopsea var. pyrenaica
intro: Gymnadenia conopsea var. pyrenaica, also known as the Pyrenean fragrant orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the fragrant orchid plant, native to the Pyrenees mountain range in Europe. Traditionally, Gymnadenia conopsea var. pyrenaica has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a potential treatment for colds and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, Gymnadenia conopsea var. pyrenaica is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is largely limited due to its rarity and restricted cultivation.
